result:
Final state: build is green; the original failure at line 274/275 (the assertion `X == true` after a `goto` jumping back out of scope of a `<close>` variable) is resolved. The test now hits a different downstream assertion, which is a separate bug.

**Root cause:** `getlocalattribute` (crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs:4059) was a Phase-A placeholder that always returned `VarKind::Reg`, ignoring `<const>` and `<close>` attributes. As a consequence, `localstat` never set `toclose`, `checktoclose` never called `marktobeclosed`, and (compounded) `checktoclose` had its `OP_TBC` emission commented out. Together these meant `local a <close> = â€¦` was compiled as a regular local: no TBC mark, no `bl.upval=true`, no `__close` invocation when control left the block via `goto`.

**Fix:** In `getlocalattribute`, compare `attr_name.as_bytes()` against `b"const"` / `b"close"` and return the matching `VarKind`, with a syntax error for unknown attributes. In `checktoclose`, emit the previously-stubbed `OP_TBC` instruction via `emit_inst` after `marktobeclosed`.

**New state:** The previously-failing assertion at combined line 275 (= original 266, the `<close>` upvalue-closing test) no longer fires. The program now fails at a later, location-less `assertion failed!` â€” a separate bug for the next debug iteration. Build (`cargo build -q -p lua-cli`) is green; no temporary instrumentation was added.
